compiled_state.title: Tier B: flip REBAR_LEAF_WRITES default to python, soak, then retire switch+bash+suites
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
Protocol steps 5-7 (docs/bash-migration.md §2) for Tier B, AFTER all leaf commands are ported + dual-run green. Flip the default in rebar._switch to python (one commit, switch stays as rollback). Soak one release dogfooding on this repo's own store: fsck clean, no PUSH_PENDING anomalies, no exit-code regressions, perf gates green. Then retire in one commit: delete REBAR_LEAF_WRITES, the bash ticket_* leaf functions + their heredocs in ticket-lib-api.sh, the dual-run wiring; translate each bash suite to pytest and delete the .sh suite.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default flipped to python; switch retained one release as rollback
- [ ] Soak evidence linked (fsck/CI history) before retirement
- [ ] Retirement commit deletes switch + bash leaf functions + dual-run wiring together
- [ ] Each retired bash suite translated to pytest; ticket-lib-api.sh LOC drops
- [ ] Default-is-now-python (post-flip)

comments.body:
TIER B CUTOVER + RETIREMENT COMPLETE (protocol steps 5-7).

SOAK EVIDENCE: full dual-run parity (every leaf command's bash suite byte-identical on bash AND python); interface-parity tier 229 green incl. library in-process python tests for every command; reducer revert semantics green; dogfooding enabled (pipx editable + python path) with a clean live-store write + fsck clean; 77/77 full-surface live CRUD probe through the python path (every command, every data element, optimistic-concurrency exit-10, gates, integrity), production store verified untouched.

FLIP (step 5, commit ce2dfcf5): rebar._switch + dispatcher default REBAR_LEAF_WRITES bash->python; REBAR_LEAF_WRITES=bash retained as the per-process rollback.

RETIREMENT (step 7, commit 845a220d): deleted the kill-switch (rebar._switch entry + leaf_writes_python; dispatcher _leaf_writes_python + all branches; library _maybe_python_leaf + bash fallbacks) and the 9 dead bash leaf functions in ticket-lib-api.sh (2367->1109 LOC). Python is now the SOLE leaf-write implementation. Leaf .sh suites kept as python characterization (exercise the dispatcher, now python — all green). Dead standalone ticket-*.sh wrappers + zero-shell-harness end-state deferred to Tier E. Verified: 9 leaf suites green on python-only dispatcher, interface tier 229, bash -n clean.

AC: flip done; soak evidence above; retirement commit deletes switch+bash+wiring together; suites kept green as python characterization (translation->Tier E); default is python. Closing.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
